confirmation.detail: inbox envelope env_e377c8f9-ea1d-4d0a-b6cb-50a9a3949838 read back as archived

incident.observed_defect: OSM delivery occurred while Operator was actively typing, causing disruptive focus/input behavior.
incident.operator_assessment: Defect; solution unclear. Needs principled delivery safety model.

payload.open_design_question: When target is on another Windows desktop, should OSM summon the target surface to the current desktop for delivery instead of switching desktops? Initial hypothesis: safer for preserving Operator context, but must be visible, reversible, and policy-gated.
proposed_capa: Introduce an OSM delivery admission gate that distinguishes idle, active-typing, and explicit-interrupt states before any focus/window mutation.
Default policy should refuse or queue delivery while recent operator keyboard/mouse activity is detected, unless the message is declared urgent and authorized for interruption.
Delivery should return a promise/receipt state such as queued_waiting_for_idle, delivered, expired, or fallback_to_inbox rather than forcing immediate delivery.

For cross-desktop delivery, evaluate a summon-to-current-desktop strategy as a safer alternative to switching the Operator's desktop; this should be explicit because moving/summoning a window is a visible workspace mutation.
Add tests or simulation fixtures for operator typing during OSM delivery, same-desktop delivery, cross-desktop delivery, expiry fallback, and focus restoration.
payload.requested_disposition: Treat as CAPA and design/implementation proposal for OSM delivery safety.
risk: Partial operator input may be split, misdirected, or submitted in the wrong surface.
Operator loses trust in OSM because background delivery can seize attention at arbitrary times.
Cross-desktop delivery may amplify the issue by changing context while the Operator is mid-thought.
payload.summary: While the Operator was typing in the current window, an operator-surface message delivery occurred and injected text/focus activity into another surface. This interrupted active human input. The existing input escrow/focus-restore behavior is insufficient because it treats delivery as safe once target resolution succeeds, rather than first proving the Operator is idle or explicitly permitting interruption.
payload.title: CAPA: OSM delivery must not interrupt active operator typing

promotion.note: Envelope archived; no target-zone mutation was performed.
